title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: vignette reproduces real-data analysis paper “Functional Principal Components Analysis Locally Adaptive Smoothing” using afpca package. apply Adaptive Functional Principal Components Analysis (Adaptive FPCA) trial-averaged neural firing rate data recorded reaching task, compare results standard fpca.sc method refund package.

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
code: library(afpca) library(refund) require(tidyverse) require(ggplot2) require(tidyfun) require(patchwork)  data(firing_rates_data)

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: firing_rates_data contains trial-averaged mean activation profiles (spikes/sec) 25 neurons 1.74-second window. row one neuron, activation column stores firing rate profile tfd functional data object. time axis shifted t=0t = 0 corresponds cue onset. neuron-level averages displayed Panel B1 Figure 1 paper.
code: firing_rates_data |>   tf_unnest(activation) |>   mutate(activation_arg = activation_arg - 0.25) |>   ggplot(aes(x = activation_arg, y = activation_value, group = neuron_no)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.5) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation (spikes / sec)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 12))

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: Figure 4 compares Adaptive FPCA fpca.sc across two panels: Panel shows first two estimated FPCs model, displayed estimated mean function (black) perturbed 25th (red) 75th (blue) percentiles corresponding scores. Panel B shows observed vs. reconstructed activation profiles four representative neurons.

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: reshape functional data numeric matrix (neurons × timepoints), fit models, flip FPC signs dominant pattern reflects increased post-cue activation.
code: Y.data <- firing_rates_data |>   tf_spread(activation) |>   select(-neuron_no) |>   as.matrix()  # Adaptive FPCA Fit.Adaptive.FPCA <- fpca.adapt(data = t(Y.data), nbs = 40, n.comp = 15) Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs   * -1 Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores <- Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores * -1  # Standard FPCA Fit.sc <- fpca.sc(Y = Y.data, nbasis = 40) Fit.sc$efunctions <- Fit.sc$efunctions * -1 Fit.sc$scores     <- Fit.sc$scores     * -1

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: helper make_fpc_df() builds tidy data frame single FPC, storing mean mean +/- FPC.
code: time_grid <- 1:174 / 100 - 0.25  make_fpc_df <- function(mu, phi, scores, fpc_no, model) {   data.frame(     Mu    = mu,     plus  = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.75),     minus = mu + phi * quantile(scores, 0.25),     time  = time_grid,     fpc_no = fpc_no,     model  = model   ) }  fpcs_data <- bind_rows(   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 1],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$mean, Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$fpcs[, 2],               Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "Adaptive FPCA"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 1],               Fit.sc$scores[, 1], "Activation Pattern 1", "fpca.sc"),   make_fpc_df(Fit.sc$mu, Fit.sc$efunctions[, 2],               Fit.sc$scores[, 2], "Activation Pattern 2", "fpca.sc") )  figure4_panelA <- fpcs_data |>   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = Mu),    colour = "black") +   geom_line(aes(y = plus),  colour = "blue") +   geom_line(aes(y = minus), colour = "red") +   facet_grid(model ~ fpc_no) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure4_panelA

title: Reproducibility - Real Data Analysis
text: helper make_neuron_df() pairs observed model-reconstructed profiles single neuron. four representative neurons model.
code: make_neuron_df <- function(idx, label, model_name, observed_mat, fitted_mat) {   data.frame(     observed = observed_mat[, idx],     fitted   = fitted_mat[, idx],     time     = time_grid,     model    = model_name,     neuron   = label   ) }  neuron_idx    <- c(1, 4, 15, 25) neuron_labels <- paste("Neuron", 1:4)  neurons_data <- bind_rows(   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "Adaptive FPCA", list(t(Y.data)), list(Fit.Adaptive.FPCA$Y_hat)),   Map(make_neuron_df, neuron_idx, neuron_labels,       "fpca.sc",       list(t(Y.data)), list(t(Fit.sc$Yhat))) ) |> bind_rows()  figure4_panelB <- neurons_data |>   ggplot(aes(x = time)) +   geom_line(aes(y = observed, colour = "Observed"),      alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(aes(y = fitted,   colour = "Reconstructed")) +   facet_grid(model ~ neuron) +   labs(x = "Time from cue (sec)", y = "Mean Activation\n(spikes / sec)") +   scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = "purple", "Reconstructed" = "black")) +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11), legend.title = element_blank())  figure4_panelB

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
text: vignette presents small-scale version simulation study paper. compare Adaptive FPCA (fpca.adapt) two standard methods refund package — fpca.sc fpca.face — across settings vary number subjects residual noise variance. full simulations paper used 100 replications per scenario three sample sizes ∈{25,50,100}\\{25, 50, 100\}; vignette uses 10 replications two sample sizes ∈{15,25}\\{15, 25\} keep runtime manageable, original simulations took around 8+ hours run using 10 cores intermediate output saved monitor convergence, resulting 2GB+ data. conclusions remain . Note: replicate exact results paper, set n_sim = 100, N.subj = c(25, 50, 100), nbs = 40.

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: library(afpca) library(refund) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(patchwork)

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
text: package provides simulate_adaptive_functional_data(), generates functional curves spatially heterogeneous smoothness — setting Adaptive FPCA expected outperform standard methods. true data-generating process two functional principal components built sinusoidal functions varying period amplitude. plot shows one example dataset (=25I = 25, σϵ2=0.1\sigma^2_\epsilon = 0.1).
code: example_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(   n.tp      = 100,   N.subj    = 25,   noise.var = 0.1,   seed.num  = 1 )  mean_df <- data.frame(   timepoint = seq_len(nrow(example_data$data)),   value     = example_data$Mu_true )  as.data.frame(example_data$data) |>   mutate(timepoint = row_number()) |>   pivot_longer(-timepoint, names_to = "subject", values_to = "value") |>   ggplot(aes(x = timepoint, y = value)) +   geom_line(aes(group = subject), colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(data = mean_df, aes(colour = "True mean"), linewidth = 1.2) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("True mean" = "blue")) +   labs(x = "Timepoint", y = "Value", colour = NULL) +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 12))

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
text: vary two factors: Number subjects ∈{15,25}\\{15, 25\} Noise variance σϵ2∈{0.1,0.2}\sigma^2_\epsilon \\{0.1, 0.2\} scenario run n_sim = 10 independent replications. replication simulate data, fit three models, record: ISE (Integrated Squared Error) estimated mean first two FPCs. Curve-specific MISE (Mean ISE averaged subjects). Number FPCs selected method. Since FPC signs arbitrary, align estimated FPC true FPC computing ISE. also store full estimated functions scenario =25I = 25, σϵ2=0.1\sigma^2_\epsilon = 0.1 produce Figure 2.
code: # ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  align_sign <- function(est, true) if (sum(est * true) < 0) -est else est ise        <- function(est, true) sum((est - true)^2) / length(true)  # ── Settings ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  sim_grid <- expand.grid(   N.subj    = c(15, 25),   noise.var = c(0.1, 0.2) )  n_sim      <- 10 n.tp       <- 100 nbs        <- 30 argvals    <- seq(0, 1, length.out = n.tp) timepoints <- seq_len(n.tp) / 100  method_colors <- c("Adaptive FPCA" = "darkorange",                    "fpca.face"     = "skyblue2",                    "fpca.sc"       = "dodgerblue3")  # Storage for summary metrics (all scenarios) all_results <- vector("list", nrow(sim_grid))  # Storage for full function estimates (Figure 2 scenario only) fig2_N.subj    <- 25 fig2_noise.var <- 0.2 fig2_runs      <- vector("list", n_sim)  # ── Run simulations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────  for (j in seq_len(nrow(sim_grid))) {    N.subj    <- sim_grid$N.subj[j]   noise.var <- sim_grid$noise.var[j]   is_fig2   <- (N.subj == fig2_N.subj & noise.var == fig2_noise.var)    scenario_results <- vector("list", n_sim)    for (s in seq_len(n_sim)) {      sim_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(       n.tp      = n.tp,       N.subj    = N.subj,       noise.var = noise.var,       seed.num  = s     )      # Fit models     fit_adapt <- fpca.adapt(data = sim_data, nbs = nbs, n.comp = 10, pve = 0.99)     fit_sc    <- fpca.sc(Y = t(sim_data$data), nbasis = nbs)     fit_face  <- fpca.face(Y = t(sim_data$data), argvals = argvals)      # Align FPC signs to truth     a_fpc1 <- align_sign(fit_adapt$fpcs[, 1],      sim_data$Phi_true[, 1])     a_fpc2 <- align_sign(fit_adapt$fpcs[, 2],      sim_data$Phi_true[, 2])          # Rescale fpca.sc efunctions to match continuous L2 norm before aligning     sc_efun <- qr.Q(qr(fit_sc$efunctions))     s_fpc1 <- align_sign(sc_efun[, 1], sim_data$Phi_true[, 1])     s_fpc2 <- align_sign(sc_efun[, 2], sim_data$Phi_true[, 2])          #     f_fpc1 <- align_sign(fit_face$efunctions[, 1], sim_data$Phi_true[, 1])     f_fpc2 <- align_sign(fit_face$efunctions[, 2], sim_data$Phi_true[, 2])      # Curve-specific MISE     adapt_subj <- mean(sapply(seq_len(N.subj), function(i)       ise(fit_adapt$Y_hat[, i],  sim_data$data_true[, i])))     sc_subj    <- mean(sapply(seq_len(N.subj), function(i)       ise(t(fit_sc$Yhat)[, i],   sim_data$data_true[, i])))     face_subj  <- mean(sapply(seq_len(N.subj), function(i)       ise(t(fit_face$Yhat)[, i], sim_data$data_true[, i])))      # Summary metrics     scenario_results[[s]] <- tibble(       sim       = s,       N.subj    = N.subj,       noise.var = noise.var,       `Adaptive FPCA_Mean`    = ise(fit_adapt$mean, sim_data$Mu_true),       `Adaptive FPCA_FPC 1`   = ise(a_fpc1,         sim_data$Phi_true[, 1]),       `Adaptive FPCA_FPC 2`   = ise(a_fpc2,         sim_data$Phi_true[, 2]),       `fpca.sc_Mean`          = ise(fit_sc$mu,       sim_data$Mu_true),       `fpca.sc_FPC 1`         = ise(s_fpc1,          sim_data$Phi_true[, 1]),       `fpca.sc_FPC 2`         = ise(s_fpc2,          sim_data$Phi_true[, 2]),       `fpca.face_Mean`        = ise(fit_face$mu,     sim_data$Mu_true),       `fpca.face_FPC 1`       = ise(f_fpc1,          sim_data$Phi_true[, 1]),       `fpca.face_FPC 2`       = ise(f_fpc2,          sim_data$Phi_true[, 2]),       `Adaptive FPCA_Subject` = adapt_subj,       `fpca.sc_Subject`       = sc_subj,       `fpca.face_Subject`     = face_subj,       adapt_nfpc = ncol(fit_adapt$fpcs),       sc_nfpc    = ncol(fit_sc$efunctions),       face_nfpc  = ncol(fit_face$efunctions)     )      # Store full function estimates for Figure 2 scenario     if (is_fig2) {       fig2_runs[[s]] <- list(         sim        = s,         Y          = sim_data$data,         data_true  = sim_data$data_true,         Mu_true    = sim_data$Mu_true,         Phi_true   = sim_data$Phi_true,         adapt_mean = fit_adapt$mean, adapt_fpc1 = a_fpc1, adapt_fpc2 = a_fpc2,         adapt_Yhat = fit_adapt$Y_hat,         sc_mean    = fit_sc$mu,      sc_fpc1    = s_fpc1, sc_fpc2    = s_fpc2,         sc_Yhat    = t(fit_sc$Yhat),         face_mean  = fit_face$mu,    face_fpc1  = f_fpc1, face_fpc2  = f_fpc2,         face_Yhat  = t(fit_face$Yhat)       )     }   }    all_results[[j]] <- bind_rows(scenario_results)   message("Scenario ", j, " / ", nrow(sim_grid), " complete") }  all_results <- bind_rows(all_results)

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
text: Figure 2 zooms single scenario examine estimation quality detail: Panel shows estimated mean FPCs across replications (black, semi-transparent) overlaid truth (red). Panel B shows observed data (grey), true underlying curve (dark grey), method’s fitted curve two representative subjects.

what: Panel A: Estimated Functions Across Replications
title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: make_functions_df <- function(run) {   bind_rows(     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$adapt_mean,                component = "Mean",  method = "Adaptive FPCA", sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$adapt_fpc1,                component = "FPC 1", method = "Adaptive FPCA", sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$adapt_fpc2,                component = "FPC 2", method = "Adaptive FPCA", sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$sc_mean,                component = "Mean",  method = "fpca.sc",       sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$sc_fpc1,                component = "FPC 1", method = "fpca.sc",       sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$sc_fpc2,                component = "FPC 2", method = "fpca.sc",       sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$face_mean,                component = "Mean",  method = "fpca.face",     sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$face_fpc1,                component = "FPC 1", method = "fpca.face",     sim = run$sim),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$face_fpc2,                component = "FPC 2", method = "fpca.face",     sim = run$sim)   ) }  panelA_est <- bind_rows(lapply(fig2_runs, make_functions_df)) |>   mutate(     method    = factor(method, levels = c("Adaptive FPCA", "fpca.sc", "fpca.face")),     component = factor(component, levels = c("Mean", "FPC 1", "FPC 2"),                        labels = c(expression(mu(t)),                                   expression(phi[1](t)),                                   expression(phi[2](t))))   )  ref_run <- fig2_runs[[1]] panelA_true <- expand.grid(     method    = c("Adaptive FPCA", "fpca.sc", "fpca.face"),     component = c("Mean", "FPC 1", "FPC 2"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) |>   rowwise() |>   mutate(data = list(data.frame(     index = timepoints,     value = switch(component,                    "Mean"  = ref_run$Mu_true,                    "FPC 1" = ref_run$Phi_true[, 1],                    "FPC 2" = ref_run$Phi_true[, 2])   ))) |>   unnest(data) |>   mutate(     method    = factor(method, levels = c("Adaptive FPCA", "fpca.sc", "fpca.face")),     component = factor(component, levels = c("Mean", "FPC 1", "FPC 2"),                        labels = c(expression(mu(t)),                                   expression(phi[1](t)),                                   expression(phi[2](t))))   )  figure2_panelA <- panelA_est |>   ggplot(aes(x = index, y = value)) +   geom_line(aes(group = sim), colour = "black", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_line(data = panelA_true, colour = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +   facet_grid(method ~ component, scales = "free_y",            labeller = labeller(component = label_parsed, method = label_value)) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1)) +   labs(x = "Time", y = "") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure2_panelA

what: Panel B: Observed vs. Fitted for Two Subjects
title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: run      <- fig2_runs[[1]] subj_idx <- c(1, 2)  make_subject_df <- function(idx, label, run) {   bind_rows(     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$Y[, idx],                type = "Observed",  method = "Adaptive FPCA"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$data_true[, idx],                type = "True",      method = "Adaptive FPCA"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$Y[, idx],                type = "Observed",  method = "fpca.sc"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$data_true[, idx],                type = "True",      method = "fpca.sc"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$Y[, idx],                type = "Observed",  method = "fpca.face"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$data_true[, idx],                type = "True",      method = "fpca.face"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$adapt_Yhat[, idx],                type = "Fitted",    method = "Adaptive FPCA"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$sc_Yhat[, idx],                type = "Fitted",    method = "fpca.sc"),     data.frame(index = timepoints, value = run$face_Yhat[, idx],                type = "Fitted",    method = "fpca.face")   ) |> mutate(subject = label) }  panelB_data <- bind_rows(   make_subject_df(subj_idx[1], "Subject 1", run),   make_subject_df(subj_idx[2], "Subject 2", run) ) |>   mutate(method = factor(method,                          levels = c("Adaptive FPCA", "fpca.sc", "fpca.face")))  figure2_panelB <- panelB_data |>   ggplot(aes(x = index, y = value)) +   geom_line(data = \(d) filter(d, type != "Fitted"),             aes(group = type), colour = "black", alpha = 0.5) +   geom_line(data = \(d) filter(d, type == "Fitted"),             colour = "blue", linewidth = 0.8) +   facet_grid(method ~ subject, scales = "free_y") +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1)) +   labs(x = "Time", y = "", colour = "Method") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11), legend.position = "none")  figure2_panelB

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
text: Figure 3 summarises estimation accuracy across simulation scenarios. Panel shows ISE mean first two FPCs. Panel B shows curve-specific MISE. Panel C shows number FPCs selected method.

what: Panel A: ISE for Mean and FPCs
title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: ise_long <- all_results |>   select(sim, N.subj, noise.var,          starts_with("Adaptive FPCA_"),          starts_with("fpca.sc_"),          starts_with("fpca.face_")) |>   pivot_longer(     cols      = -c(sim, N.subj, noise.var),     names_to  = c("method", "type"),     names_sep = "_"   ) |>   filter(type != "Subject") |>   mutate(     N.subj    = as.factor(N.subj),     noise.var = factor(noise.var,                        labels = c(expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.1),                                   expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.2))),     type      = factor(type, levels = c("Mean", "FPC 1", "FPC 2"),                        labels = c(expression(mu(t)),                                   expression(phi[1](t)),                                   expression(phi[2](t))))   )  figure1_panelA <- ise_long |>   ggplot(aes(x = N.subj, y = value, colour = method)) +   geom_boxplot() +   facet_grid(noise.var ~ type, labeller = label_parsed) +   scale_colour_manual(values = method_colors) +   labs(x = "Number of Subjects (I)", y = "ISE", colour = "Method") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure1_panelA

title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: mise_long <- all_results |>   select(sim, N.subj, noise.var,          `Adaptive FPCA_Subject`, `fpca.sc_Subject`, `fpca.face_Subject`) |>   pivot_longer(     cols      = -c(sim, N.subj, noise.var),     names_to  = c("method", "type"),     names_sep = "_"   ) |>   mutate(     N.subj    = as.factor(N.subj),     noise.var = factor(noise.var,                        labels = c(expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.1),                                   expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.2)))   )  figure1_panelB <- mise_long |>   ggplot(aes(x = N.subj, y = value, colour = method)) +   geom_boxplot() +   facet_grid(noise.var ~ ., labeller = label_parsed) +   scale_colour_manual(values = method_colors) +   labs(x = "Number of Subjects (I)", y = "Curve-Specific MISE", colour = "Method") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure1_panelB

what: Panel C: Number of FPCs Selected
title: Reproducibility - Simulations
code: nfpc_long <- all_results |>   select(sim, N.subj, noise.var, adapt_nfpc, sc_nfpc, face_nfpc) |>   pivot_longer(     cols      = c(adapt_nfpc, sc_nfpc, face_nfpc),     names_to  = "method",     values_to = "n_fpc"   ) |>   mutate(     N.subj    = as.factor(N.subj),     method    = recode(method, "adapt_nfpc" = "Adaptive FPCA",                                "sc_nfpc"    = "fpca.sc",                                "face_nfpc"  = "fpca.face"),     noise.var = factor(noise.var,                        labels = c(expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.1),                                   expression(sigma[epsilon]^2 == 0.2)))   )  figure1_panelC <- nfpc_long |>   ggplot(aes(x = N.subj, y = n_fpc, colour = method)) +   geom_boxplot() +   facet_grid(noise.var ~ ., labeller = label_parsed) +   scale_colour_manual(values = method_colors) +   labs(x = "Number of Subjects (I)", y = "Selected Number of FPCs (K)",        colour = "Method") +   theme_bw() +   theme(text = element_text(size = 11))  figure1_panelC

code: @Manual{,   title = {afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis},   author = {Angel {Garcia de la Garza} and Britton Sauerbrei and Jeff Goldsmith},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9},   url = {https://github.com/angelgar/afpca}, }

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis Authors: Angel Garcia de la Garza, Britton Sauerbrei, Jeff Goldsmith License: MIT. See LICENSE file details Version: 0.9

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: afpca implements Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis (aFPCA), method estimating directions variation functional data exhibit sharp changes smoothness. Standard FPCA methods impose global smoothness assumption can fail capture abrupt transitions underlying signal. afpca addresses combining fast scalable adaptive scatterplot smoothing technique probabilistic FPCA framework, allowing functional principal components smoothed adaptively. particularly useful applications neural recordings, sharp changes activity following stimulus must distinguished smooth baseline behavior.

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: can install development version afpca GitHub :
code: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("angelgar/afpca")

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: examples running adaptive FPCA. details use package can found XYZ. code uses function afpca::simulate_adaptive_functional_data() simulate 20 curves Yi(t)Y_i(t), =1,…,20i = 1,\dots,20 observed 200 time points common grid domain (0,1) gaussian noise. functions generated mean function two functional principal components varying temporal smoothness defined : μ(t)=t−3/2\mu(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(π×t1/4)(t>1/2)\sin(\pi \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2) ϕk(t)=t−3/2\phi_k(t) = t^{-3/2}sin⁡(4π×k×t1/4)(t>1/2),k=1,2\sin(4\pi \times k \times t^{1/4})(t>1/2), k = 1,2 plot show simulated data looks like:  software performs adaptively-smoothed functional principal component analysis. main function package afpca:fpca.adapt. code illustrates : plots show estimated mean function functional principal components  plots shows two examples observed functions respective reconstructions.
code: library(afpca)  simulated_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(N.subj = 20) afpca.output <- fpca.adapt(data = simulated_data)

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: package includes vignette articles reproduce figures analyses accompanying paper. vignettes intended ensure full reproducibility results presented manuscript. vignette source files available locally within package directory ./vignettes/articles/. can also viewed online via package’s documentation site: Reproducibility Vignette – Real Data Analysis Reproducibility Vignette – Simulations

title: afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — afpca-package

what: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: dataset comes novel neuroscience research understand dynamics motor cortex activation involvement skilled movements. contains firing rates 25 neurons coming motor cortex mouse reaching food pellet. neuron-specific averages across 147 experimental trials.

title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
code: firing_rates_data

title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: data frame 25 rows two columns including: neuron_no Integer, Neuron ID activation tidyfun tfd object containing firing rates neuron across 174 timepoints, spikes / ms

title: Firing rates for 25 Neurons in the Motor Cortex — firing_rates_data
text: Sauerbrei, Britton ., et al. "Cortical pattern generation dexterous movement input-driven." Nature 577.7790 (2020): 386-391. tidyfun manual: tidyfun.github.io/tidyfun

what: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Decomposes functional observations using adaptive functional principal component analysis. method incorporates adaptive smoothness estimated decomposition using penalized likelihood framework

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: fpca.adapt(   data,   col_name = "name",   basis = "trunc.poly",   poly.degree = 2,   knots = NA,   nbs = 35,   n.comp = 18,   pve = 0.99,   normalize.scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_fpcs = TRUE,   ntimes = 200,   convergence.thresh = 1e-04 )

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: data data frame, matrix, tidyfun "tfd.reg" object functional observations. col_name Column name data. Required Y tibble. package expects column "dft" object tidyfun basis Type spline basis. "os" B-spline basis "trunc.poly" truncated polynomials (default) poly.degree degree spline basis knots Number knots used generate spline basis estimate mean function functional principal components nbs Number spline basis generate mean function functional principal components n.comp prespecified number principal components pve proportion variance explained; used choose number functional principal components normalize.scores normalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_scores orthogonalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_fpcs orthogonalize FPCs iteration (default true, recommended) ntimes Maximum number iterations convergence.thresh Threshold convergence penalized likelihood

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: function returns object class "afpca" contains following: Y observed data. Pass either matrix, "dft" object tidyverse, tibble. tibble passed, "col_name" required Y_hat reconstructed fitted values mean vector estimated mean function fpcs tp (number timepoints) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated eigenfunctions scores n (number subjects) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated scores eigenvalues estimated eigenvalues .e. variances fpc scores Basis list containing penalized (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis Theta concatenation (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis coef estimated coefficients, coef %*% theta = fpcs convergence logical, algorithm converged .iter number iterations needed convergence

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Paper

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: ## Using simulated data sim_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(n.tp = 100) afpca_output <- fpca.adapt(data = sim_data, nbs = 10)  ## Using firing rate data (uses tidyfun, install beforehand)  if (FALSE) {   data(firing_rates_data)   afpca_firing_output <- fpca.adapt(data = firing_rates_data, col_name = "activation", nbs = 10) }

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: Simulated functional data includes sharp changes domain function (.e. smoothness rapidly changes domain). data simulates data using sinusoidal functions varying period amplitude.

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
code: simulate_adaptive_functional_data(   n.tp = 200,   N.subj = 20,   var.scores = c(4, 1),   noise.var = 0.05,   seed.num = 1 )

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: n.tp number timepoints N.subj number subjects var.scores variance scores (value eigenvalues) noise.var residual noise variance seed.num seed number

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: object class "fpca_sim_data" contains following: data simulated dataset data_true simulated dataset without noise Mu_true true data-generating mean-function Phi_true true data-generating functional principal components Scores_true true scores

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
code: sim_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(n.tp = 100, N.subj = 25)
